ORDER AFTER HEARING ON DEMURRER

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANT ADMIRAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. ADMIRAL INSURANCE COMPANY RAISES A QUESTION OF FACT WHETHER CLARENDON AMERICA INSURANCE COMPANY WAS NOT PRIMARILY LIABLE FOR THE CLAIMED LOSS, WHICH IS NOT THE PROPER SUBJECT OF A DEMURRER. (TRANSCONTINENTAL INS. CO. V. INS. CO. OF THE ST. OF PENN. (2007) 148 CAL.APP.4TH 1296, 1307 - 1308.) FURTHER, THE DOCTRINE OF EQUITABLE SUBROGATION "IS BROAD ENOUGH TO INCLUDE EVERY INSTANCE IN WHICH ONE PERSON, NOT ACTING AS A MERE VOLUNTEER OR INTRUDER, PAYS A DEBT FOR WHICH ANOTHER IS PRIMARILY LIABLE, AND WHICH IN EQUITY AND GOOD CONSCIENCE SHOULD HAVE BEEN DISCHARGED BY THE LATTER." (ID. AT 1305.) THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS UNOPPOSED, AND THUS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON OCT-25-2013 IN 16, DEFENDANT ADMIRAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SUBROGATION IS GRANTED. PARAGRAPHS 22 AND 30 OF THE COMPLAINT IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION DO NOT UNEQUIVOCALLY ESTABLISH THAT THE PLAINTIFF IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION SOUGHT RELIEF ONLY FOR PROPERTY DAMAGE WHICH OCCURRED AFTER THE EXPIRATION OF THE PERIOD THAT ADMIRAL INSURED ALLIED. PARAGRAPH 22 OF THAT COMPLAINT, RELIED ON BY ADMIRAL, STATES THAT 'ASSOCIATION IS PRESENTLY UNAWARE OF WHEN' THE DAMAGE 'FIRST OCCURRED.' LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, PARAGRAPH 30 OF THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT CAN BE FAIRLY READ AS ALLEGING THAT COVERED PROPERTY DAMAGE OCCURRED DURING THE POLICY PERIOD. PER PEPPERELL V. SCOTTSDALE (1998) 62 CAL APP. 4TH 1045, ADMIRAL HAS NOT ELIMINATED THE POSSIBILITY OF A COVERED DAMAGE DURING THE ADMIRAL POLICY PERIOD AND THUS IS NOT ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR. HOWEVER, ADMIRAL SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE CLAIMS IN THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT ALLEGING A VIOLATION OF THE CC&RS AND A BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY ARE OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF COVERAGE OF THE ADMIRAL POLICY AND CLARENDON HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY FACTS RAISING A TRIABLE ISSUE ON THIS POINT. THEREFORE, PER TRANSCONTINENTAL INSURANCE CO. V. INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE STATE OF PENNSYLVANIA (2007) 148 CAL. APP. 1296, THERE IS NO MERIT TO CLARENDON'S CLAIM FOR SUBROGATION AND THUS ADMIRAL IS ENTITLED TO SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN ITS FAVOR ON THAT CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE HAROLD E. KAHN, CSR LAVENA WARD. (D29)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Change Of Hearing Date
Off Calendar Oct-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Atty. Henry Su of Yaron & Associates appear for plaintiff and opposing Clarendon America Insurance. / Atty. James Derfler of Walsh McKean Furcolo appear for defendant moving party Admiral Insurance Company. / Atty Jennifer Elowsky of Wolkin & Curran appear for defenant American Safety Industry Company.

Argued, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Admiral's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication as to the fourth cause of action for subrogation is granted. Paragraphs 22 and 30 of the complaint in the underlying action do not unequivocally establish that the plaintiff in the underlying action sought relief only for property damage which occurred after the expiration of the period that Admiral insured Allied. Paragraph 22 of that complaint, relied on by Admiral, states that 'ASSOCIATION is presently unaware of when' the damage 'first occurred.' Liberally construed, paragraph 30 of the underlying complaint can be fairly read as alleging that covered property damage occurred during the policy period. Per Pepperell v. Scottsdale (1998) 62 Cal App. 4th 1045, Admiral has not eliminated the possibility of a covered damage during the Admiral policy period and thus is not entitled to summary judgment in its favor. However, Admiral satisfied its burden of showing that the claims in the underlying complaint alleging a violation of the CC&Rs and a breach of fiduciary duty are outside the scope of coverage of the Admiral policy and Clarendon has not presented any facts raising a triable issue on this point. Therefore, per Transcontinental Insurance Co. v. Insurance Company of the State of Pennsylvania (2007) 148 Cal. App. 1296, there is no merit to Clarendon's claim for subrogation and thus Admiral is entitled to summary adjudication in its favor on that claim. Order signed in open court. Judge Harold E. Kahn, CSR LaVena Ward. (D29)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stephen M. Kerins, Esq. of Walsh McKean Furcolo LLP, Tel. 619.232.8486 x1023, for Defendant and moving party via court call;
Henry M. Su, Esq. of Yaron & Associates, Tel. 415.658.2929, for Plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted the tentative ruling in its entirety. DEFENDANT ADMIRAL INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Admiral Insurance Company raises a question of fact whether Clarendon America Insurance Company was not primarily liable for the claimed loss, which is not the proper subject of a demurrer. (Transcontinental Ins. Co. v. Ins. Co. of the St. of Penn. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 1296, 1307 - 1308.) Further, the doctrine of equitable subrogation "is broad enough to include every instance in which one person, not acting as a mere volunteer or intruder, pays a debt for which another is primarily liable, and which in equity and good conscience should have been discharged by the latter." (Id. at 1305.) The Request for Judicial Notice is unopposed, and thus granted. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
